title: Design ads CTR prediction at scale + DSA review

items.label: Kth Largest Element IN A Stream
Design ad CTR prediction at scale
interviewQuestions: Walk me through designing Meta's ad CTR prediction system.
How do you handle billion-cardinality features (user_id, ad_id) at scale?

items.label: Feature engineering for CTR (cross features, embeddings)
interviewQuestions: How do cross features (e.g., user × ad) work, and how do you scale them?
When does DCN / DeepFM beat hand-crafted cross features in production?

items.label: Calibration & bid optimization
interviewQuestions: Why is calibration critical for ad ranking, and how do you evaluate it?
How would you recalibrate after a model retrain without disturbing existing bid logic?

items.label: Clarify requirements, users, constraints, and business metric for: Design ads CTR prediction at scale
interviewQuestions: What are the first five clarifying questions before proposing any model?
What is the business metric, what is the ML proxy metric, and where can they diverge?
What scale, latency, freshness, privacy, and reliability constraints change the architecture?

items.label: Defend data, labels, baseline, model choice, feature freshness, and serving path for: Design ads CTR prediction at scale
interviewQuestions: What baseline would you launch before a complex model, and how would you know it is insufficient?
Where do labels come from, what bias do they contain, and how delayed are they?
Which features must be computed online vs offline, and what happens when the feature store is slow?

items.label: Cover monitoring, rollback, cost, security, failure modes, and launch plan for: Design ads CTR prediction at scale
interviewQuestions: What online metrics, guardrails, and cohort slices decide whether this system is safe to ramp?
What is the rollback trigger, fallback experience, and incident owner if quality or latency regresses?
What are the top security, privacy, abuse, or cost risks, and what controls would you ship in v1?
